title: Positional Joins broken by #19566
description:
### What happens?

Positional joins incorrectly return 0 rows in certain cases when reading parquet files after #19566

This bug was revealed when trying to run ducklake with main which runs the test suite using parquet files as the basis for tables rather than in memory.

@carlopi

### To Reproduce

Taken from test/sql/join/positional/test_positional_join.test but using parquet files rather than in memory tables

```sql
CREATE TABLE two (a INTEGER, b INTEGER);
INSERT INTO two VALUES (11, 1), (12, 2);
COPY two TO 'two.parquet';
DROP TABLE two;
CREATE VIEW two AS FROM 'two.parquet';
SELECT * FROM two t1 POSITIONAL JOIN two t2;
```

### OS:

Linux x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

v1.5.0-dev2458

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Julian Meyers

### Affiliation:

Independent

- [x] Yes, I have

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Not applicable - the reproduction does not require a data set
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE three (x INT, y VARCHAR(255), z FLOAT, w BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO three VALUES (1, 'test', 1.1, TRUE), (2, 'example', 2.2, FALSE);
SELECT * FROM three INTO OUTFILE 'three.parquet' FIELDS TERMINATED BY ',' ENCLOSED BY '"' LINES TERMINATED BY '\n';
CREATE TABLE three AS SELECT * FROM CSVREAD('three.parquet');
key_bug_features: Positional joins return 0 rows with parquet files
